title: Bruins’ prospect James Hagens turns pro, will begin career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, a standout BC forward, joins Providence Bruins on an AHL amateur tryout agreement, ending his collegiate career.
Hagens, the seventh overall pick in 2025 NHL Draft, will practice with Providence Bruins and may play against Springfield Thunderbirds.
BC head coach Greg Brown praises Hagens' growth; Bruins eager to integrate him for potential NHL lineup impact.

title: 7 X-factors on 2026 Red Sox roster
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer's development is crucial; he must overcome last season's struggles at the plate for Red Sox success.
Roman Anthony is a key player with MVP potential, crucial for Boston's offense from the leadoff spot.
Brayan Bello aims to bolster the starting rotation by maintaining his spring training performance into the season.

title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: State Rep. Bradley Jones, Jr., Massachusetts House Minority Leader, will not seek reelection after 32 years in office.
Jones, who was first sworn in 1994, will continue serving the 20th Middlesex District until January 2027.
Amy Carnevale commended Jones's dedication, stating his service left a lasting impact on the Republican Party in Massachusetts.

title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: No ICE agents were deployed to Logan Airport on March 23, 2026, according to TSA union president Mike Gayzagian.
DHS officers were present at Logan for a routine security operation, not related to ICE deployments.
ICE agents have been sent to 14 airports nationwide due to increased wait times, while Logan has avoided such delays.

title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez has been on paid leave since February 26, pending an external investigation.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty is serving as acting chief during Chief Suarez's leave, according to Mayor Sam Squailia.
The city has not disclosed details of the investigation, citing it as a personnel matter.

title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was seriously injured in a stabbing outside 72 Seaport Blvd, Boston on early Sunday morning.
The victim, found with abdominal and leg wounds, was treated and transported to a hospital; his status is unknown.
No arrests have been made and Boston Police continue to investigate the stabbing incident.

title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover police officer, is on trial for assault and battery with a dangerous weapon in Essex County.
Prosecutors allege Fitzsimmons aimed a gun at Officer Patrick Noonan, who then fired in self-defense, wounding her.
Fitzsimmons's defense claims she was suicidal and did not intend to harm others; her trial continues under Judge Jeffrey Karp.

title: Bruins host Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice after run to R.I. state title ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins hosted Blackstone Valley hockey team at Warrior Ice Arena following their Rhode Island state championship win.
The Blackstone Valley team, led by captain Colin Dorgan, achieved victory despite personal and community tragedies.
Bruins players Jeremy Swayman and David Pastrnak engaged with the team, taking photos and signing gear during the visit.

title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots have signed offensive tackle James Hudson, previously released by the Giants earlier this month.
Hudson has 60 career NFL games with 19 starts, primarily at right tackle, adding depth to the Patriots' offensive line.
He offers versatility with experience at both tackle positions, potentially backing up 35-year-old starter Morgan Moses.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, a Boston Bruins prospect, finished his sophomore season at Boston College and has yet to sign a pro contract.
Hagens, who recorded 47 points in 34 games, could join the Bruins or spend time in the AHL with Providence first.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm stated he's open to adding young players to the lineup if they're ready for the challenge.
